english: poison Dzl.; byi-bas ̥tsál-te (byi bas 'tshal te) eaten by mice Dzl.; ɣdon mi ̥tsál-bar (gdon mi 'tshal bar) eleg. for ɣdon mi zá-bar (gdon mi za bar) without doubt Dzl. — 3. to know Cs.; so ño-̥tsál-ba (ngo 'tshal ba) appears to be used for ño-s̀es-pa (ngo shes pa), and in a passage of S.O. it seems to imply to understand. — 4. in certain phrases: bad ̥tsál-ba (bad 'tshal ba) to use diligence Thgy.; bro ̥tsál-ba (bro 'tshal ba) 1. to swear Pth.(?), 2. to have a cold Mil.; pyag ̥tsál-ba (phyag 'tshal ba) to greet, salute, v. pyag (phyag).

pf. tsig (tshig), to burn, to destroy by fire, groṅ-k̀yer mi daṅ bcàs-pa (grong khyer mi dang bcas pa) (he burned) the town with its inhabitants Pth.; mes, mer (mes, mer), vulgo *mé-la* (*me la*) with fire; rnám-par (rnam par) entirely, completely Dzl.; more loosely: tsig soṅ (tshig song) he burnt himself, scalded himself etc.; also of food, burnt, injured by the heat; ̥tsíg-gam ('tshig gam) am I burning? (thinks one suffering of fever) Med.; of inflammation, v. mig-̥tsig (mig 'tshig); of any violent pain Dom.; to be glowing, of the evening-sky W.; *tsig ̥jug ̥dug* (*tshig 'jug 'dug*) C. to be in the rut, the copulating of larger animals.

pf. tsir (tshir), btsir (btsir), fut. ytsir (gtsir), btsir (btsir), imp. tsir (tshir) W. *btsir-c̀e* (*btsir ce*) to press, mig (mig) with the finger on the eye Med.; nán-gyis (nan gyis) to press hard Stg.; to press out, an ulcer; to wring, a wet cloth; to crush out, til-már (til mar) sesame-oil Lex.; ̥o-ma ̥tsir-ba ('o ma 'tshir ba) to milk; *tsir tag jhé-pa* (*tshir tag byed pa*), or *táṅ-wa* (*btang ba*) C. to press hard, to examine closely, to hold a rigorous inquest; btsín-mo-la (btsin mo la) yaṅ (yang) tugs (thugs) ytsir (gtsir) c̀uṅ-bar (chung bar) gyúr-to (gyur to) Pth. also the queen's mind was much depressed.

pf. tsugs (tshugs) (intrs. of ̥dzúg-pa ('dzug pa)), 1. to go into (more frq. ̥tsúd-pa ('tshud pa)), to enter upon, begin, commence, stód-pa ̥búl-ba-la tsugs (stod pa 'bul ba la tshugs) he began to praise, to flatter. — 2. to penetrate by boring, v. ṕur-pa (phur pa); to take root, to establish one's self, to settle, rtsá-ba ma tsugs (rtsa ba ma tshugs) it has not struck root; ̥brog ̥tsúgs-su ye ma-̥dod ('brog 'tshugs su ye ma 'dod) Mil., prob.: they had no longer any mind to establish themselves in this alpine solitude; brtán-gyi skyid-mgo dé-nas tsugs (brtan gyi skyid mgo de nas tshugs) this was the beginning of my lasting happiness Mil.; most frq. tsúgs-pa (tshugs pa) as partic. or adj.: firm, steady, rkaṅ-lág ma tsúgs-te sá-la ̥gyél-to (rkang lag ma tshugs te sa la 'gyel to) his limbs not remaining firm (in consequence of a paralytic stroke), he fell to the ground Dzl.; *káṅ-pa tsúg-kyin dug* (*kang pa tshug kyin dug*) sit quiet with your feet! Ld.; ̥dug mi tsúgs-pa ('dug mi tshugs pa) Med., sa gc̀ig-tu mi tsugs-pa (sa gcig tu mi tshugs pa) Pth., *dó-tsug mé-pa* (*do tshug me pa*) C., *dád-du mi tsúg-kán* (*dad du mi tshug mkhan*) W. not being able to sit still; not stationary, unsettled, roving, restless, volatile, flighty, inattentive, spyód-pas skád-c̀ig kyaṅ mi tsúgs-pa (spyod pas skad cig kyang mi tshugs pa) Glr. id.; *tsúg-la dod* (*tshug la dod*) W., be attentive! to be able C.

pf. tsud (tshud) (intrs. to ̥dzúd-pa ('dzud pa)) to be put into (a hole), to prison Glr.; to go into, to enter, to get into (a good and wholesome way), to go to (hell); koṅ-du (khong du) v. koṅ ̥tsud-pa (khong 'tshud pa).

pf. tsubs (tshubs), 1. to whirl, of whirlwinds, snow-storms, smoke etc. Mil. and elsewh. — 2. to be choked, esp. to be drowned, nya c̀ab-la ̥pyo-ba ̥tsub mi srid (nya chab la 'phyo ba 'tshub mi srid) the fish swimming in the water cannot be drowned Mil.; c̀us ̥tsúb-pa (chus 'tshub pa) Mil.; *tsub-te s̀i* (*tshub te shi*) W. he has been drowned. — 3. spyód-pa ̥tsúb-pa (spyod pa 'tshub pa) pugnacity, of fowl Glr.

1. vb. pf. btses (btses), fut. btse (btse), ytse (gtse) (Dzl.) to hurt, damage, injure, persecute, torment, mí-la ̥tsé-z̀iṅ ɣnód-pa byéd-pa (mi la 'tshe zhing gnod pa byed pa), or ɣnód-ciṅ ̥tsé-bar byéd-pa (gnod cing 'tshe bar byed pa) id.; also sbst., enemy, persecutor Mil.; ɣcàn-zán-la sógs-pai ̥tsé-ba daṅ bcàs-pa (gcan zan la sogs pahi 'tshe ba dang bcas pa) (a place) haunted by beasts of prey or any other noxious creatures Thgy.; the term is also applied to horses that bite each other. — 2. sbst. (spelling uncertain) psalterium, the third stomach of ruminating animals W.
pf. tsegs (tshegs), imp. tseg(s) (tsheg(s)), to repay Cs.
pf. prob. ̥tseṅs ('tshengs), 1. to increase, improve, thrive, opp. to ̥púṅ-ba ('phung ba) W. — 2. to be content, happy Mil.
pf. tsems (tshems), btsems (btsems), fut. btsem (btsem), imp. tsems (tshems), W. *tsem-c̀e* (*tshem ce*) to sew, *gos tsém-c̀ëi ras* (*gos tshem cëi ras*) materials for a gar-
